purpose: Constructs the two prompts every worker receives: the system prompt (worker instructions + dispatch context) and the task prompt (task description + PKI knowledge + CLAUDE.md project context + read-only additional context dirs + upstream task results). Also builds the circuit-breaker REPLAN prompt + system prompt that gets fed to a Claude CLI process when the swarm hits 3+ failures in one wave. The buildSystemPrompt function defensively validates that the dashboardId matches a known shape (`ide`, 6-char hex, or `dashboardN`) — if a master agent passed a made-up dashboardId, this throws rather than silently routing progress files into a wrong directory. PKI integration: reads `{projectPath}/.synapse/knowledge/manifest.json` and matches task title+description against domain_index, tag_index, concept_map; pulls top-8 relevant annotations and recent insights and injects them as a 'PKI Knowledge' block in the task prompt.
gotchas: DASHBOARD_ID_SHAPE = /^(ide|[a-f0-9]{6}|dashboard[0-9]+)$/ is the SOURCE OF TRUTH for valid dashboard IDs. Any ID that doesn't match throws from buildSystemPrompt — adding a new ID format requires updating this regex first.

If WORKER_INSTRUCTIONS_PATH (agent/instructions/tracker_worker_instructions.md) is missing, the system prompt falls back to a one-line 'Write progress to ...' instruction. Workers in this fallback state lack the full progress reporting protocol and tend to skip writing dashboards.
PKI knowledge is OPTIONAL and silently no-ops when manifest.json is missing — but if manifest.json is malformed, the entire prompt build still succeeds; the worker just gets no PKI block.
The gotchas section of the PKI block has NO budget cap (unlike patterns 15 / conventions 10) — a project with hundreds of gotchas would blow the prompt context.

readUpstreamResults reads `progress/<depTaskId>.json` per dependency — if a dep is reported completed in the swarm tracker but its progress file is missing, the upstream summary becomes '(progress file not found)' silently.

The replan prompt embeds the FULL agents array as JSON — for very large swarms (50+ tasks) this can balloon and exceed context; the replanner uses --output-format text (not stream-json) and is single-turn.
PKI relevance scoring: high-complexity files get +2, stale files get -1, concept matches get +2 weight. If no matches score above zero the block is omitted entirely.
patterns: Three-stage prompt construction (system / task / replan) all using a parts[] array + final parts.join('\n') idiom.
PKI auto-enrichment runs at prompt-build time — workers don't see knowledge unless their task title/description match an indexed domain/tag/concept.
Hard-coded budgets: top-8 relevant files, top-15 patterns, top-10 conventions, top-8 insights, last-5 swarm insights — keeps the PKI block under ~100 lines.
Defensive ID validation: buildSystemPrompt throws on invalid dashboardId rather than letting the worker write into the wrong dashboard.
conventions: All path tokens emitted in the prompt use backtick-wrapped absolute paths so the worker's parser/markdown can render them verbatim.

Dashboard binding fields are explicit: Synapse_Instance_Location, DashboardID, Synapse_Dashboard, Target_Directory, Context_Directories — these names appear in agent/master and worker docs and must stay stable.
Replan output schema is fixed: {summary, modified[], added[], removed[], retry[]} — see SwarmOrchestrator.applyReplan for the consumer.
